summary: This benchmark evaluates prompt compression for agentic movie generation, demonstrating a significant reduction in prompt size (75%) with acceptable quality caveats, particularly for GPT-5.5. While the methodology is sound for a no-media evaluation, the lack of public code and specific tokenizer counts limits full reproducibility.
strengths: Addresses a practical problem in LLM agent development: the cost and complexity of large prompt stacks.
Provides a clear methodology for evaluating prompt compression in a no-media agent-flow harness.
Compares performance across two major LLM providers (GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro), highlighting differences in their sensitivity to prompt changes.
Offers actionable insights into which parts of a prompt are compressible (narrative) and which are critical (schema, rubrics).
weaknesses: The supporting repository is private, hindering external reproducibility.
Approximate prompt-token estimates are used instead of provider-specific tokenizer counts.
The evaluation is limited to no-media generation, not final video quality.
Internal rubric scores are used, which are not universally standardized metrics.
suggestions: Make the supporting repository public to allow for independent verification of the benchmark methodology and results.
Incorporate provider-specific tokenizer counts for more precise measurement of prompt size reduction.
Consider a follow-up study that includes media generation to assess the impact of prompt compression on final output quality.

claims.text: The control prompt stack was approximately 145,593 editable prompt tokens.
claims.source: Web search for 'GenFlick prompt compression benchmark 145593 tokens'

claims.text: Two compact variants, first-principles-compressed-v1 and flow-overhaul-v1, reduced editable prompt text to about 37,325 and 36,595 approximate tokens respectively, a 74-75 percent reduction.
claims.source: Web search for 'GenFlick prompt compression benchmark 37325 tokens 75 percent reduction'

claims.text: The leading flow-overhaul variant reached 95.0 A+ on one first-shot lane and remained close to production on several additional cases with fewer phases.
claims.source: Web search for 'GenFlick flow-overhaul-v1 95.0 A+ first-shot lane'

claims.text: Gemini 3.1 Pro exposed sharper regressions around schema exactness, multi-shot floors, character prompt length, and staging completeness.
claims.source: Web search for 'Gemini 3.1 Pro prompt compression regressions schema exactness'

novelty_vs_existing.title: Phased Planner-Executor Architecture for Multimodal Movie Generation Agents
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission focuses specifically on prompt compression within an agentic movie generation framework, a distinct aspect from the general architecture described in 'Phased Planner-Executor Architecture'.

novelty_vs_existing.title: First-Run UX Patterns for Agentic Video Creation: State-Derived Progress, Cost Consent, and Preview Choreography
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: While related to agentic video creation, this submission's novelty lies in its benchmark of prompt compression techniques, rather than UX patterns for initial user interaction.
